title: quinquina calisaya
intro: Quinquina calisaya, also known as Cinchona calisaya, is a deciduous tree native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The bark of this plant contains a high concentration of quinine, which has been used for centuries to treat malaria and other fevers. The medicinal properties of Quinquina calisaya include its ability to reduce fever, relieve muscle and joint pain, and act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Quinquina calisaya is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing the severity of flu and cold symptoms, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as a natural anticoagulant.
